subtopics: Convert recurring decimals into fractions
Express recurring decimals in exact fractional form
topics.description: Convert recurring decimals into exact fractions using algebraic methods.

subtopics: Understand the meaning of surds
Simplify expressions involving surds
Manipulate surds including rationalising a denominator
Rationalise denominators of the form a + b√c
topics.description: Understand, simplify and manipulate surds, including rationalising denominators.

subtopics: Use index notation involving fractional powers
Use index notation involving negative and zero powers
Apply index laws to simplify numerical expressions
Evaluate expressions involving integer, fractional and negative powers
topics.description: Use index laws to simplify and evaluate numerical expressions involving integer, fractional and negative powers.

subtopics: Understand sets defined in algebraic terms and use subsets
Use Venn diagrams to represent sets and number of elements
Use n(A) notation for number of elements in set A
Use sets in practical situations
topics.description: Understand sets defined in algebraic terms, use Venn diagrams, and apply set notation including subsets and n(A).

subtopics: Calculate repeated percentage change
Solve compound interest problems
Calculate total percentage change after successive changes
topics.description: Use repeated percentage change and solve compound interest problems.

subtopics: Identify upper and lower bounds of values given to a degree of accuracy
Solve problems using upper and lower bounds
Calculate with bounds to find maximum and minimum possible values
topics.description: Solve problems using upper and lower bounds where values are given to a degree of accuracy.

subtopics: Calculate with numbers in standard form
Solve problems involving standard form in real-world contexts
topics.description: Solve problems involving standard form including calculations with very large and very small numbers.

subtopics: Expand the product of two or more linear expressions
Factorise quadratic expressions including ax² + bx + c
Manipulate algebraic fractions with numeric, linear or quadratic numerators/denominators
Complete the square for a given quadratic expression
Use algebra to support and construct proofs
topics.description: Expand products of linear expressions, factorise quadratics, manipulate algebraic fractions, complete the square, and construct proofs.

subtopics: Change the subject of a formula where the subject appears twice
Change the subject where a power of the subject occurs
Rearrange complex formulae from mathematics and real-life contexts
topics.description: Manipulate formulae to change the subject, including cases where the subject appears twice or a power of the subject occurs.

subtopics: Set up problems involving direct proportion algebraically
Set up problems involving inverse proportion algebraically
Relate algebraic solutions to graphical representations of proportion equations
topics.description: Set up problems involving direct or inverse proportion and relate algebraic solutions to graphical representations.

subtopics: Calculate the exact solution of two simultaneous linear equations in two unknowns
Interpret equations as lines and the common solution as the point of intersection
Solve simultaneous equations where one is linear and the other is quadratic
topics.description: Solve simultaneous equations in two unknowns, including one linear and one quadratic equation, and interpret geometrically.

subtopics: Solve quadratic equations by factorisation
Solve quadratic equations using the quadratic formula
Solve quadratic equations by completing the square
Form and solve quadratic equations from data given in a context
topics.description: Solve quadratic equations by factorisation, formula, and completing the square, and form equations from contextual data.

subtopics: Solve quadratic inequalities in one unknown
Represent the solution set of inequalities on a number line
Identify regions defined by harder linear inequalities on Cartesian graphs
topics.description: Solve quadratic inequalities in one unknown, represent solutions on a number line, and identify regions defined by linear inequalities.

subtopics: Understand and use common difference and first term in arithmetic sequences
Know and use the nth term formula for arithmetic sequences
Find the sum of the first n terms of an arithmetic series
topics.description: Work with arithmetic sequences using common difference and first term, find nth terms, and calculate sums of arithmetic series.

subtopics: Understand that a function is a mapping between elements of two sets
Use function notation f(x) and f: x →
Understand domain and range including values to exclude
Find composite functions fg
Find inverse functions f⁻¹
topics.description: Understand functions as mappings, use function notation, work with domain and range, and find composite and inverse functions.

subtopics: Recognise, plot and draw cubic graphs
Recognise, plot and draw reciprocal graphs
Recognise, plot and draw trigonometric graphs (sin, cos, tan) for any angle
Recognise, plot and draw circle graphs
Recognise, plot and draw exponential graphs
Apply transformations to y = f(x): f(x) + a, f(ax), f(x + a), af(x)
Interpret and analyse transformations of functions algebraically
Find gradients of non-linear graphs by drawing tangents
Find intersection points of linear and non-linear graphs
topics.description: Recognise, plot and transform graphs of cubic, reciprocal, trigonometric, circle and exponential functions, and find gradients and intersections.

subtopics: Calculate the gradient of a straight line given two points
Find the equation of a straight line parallel to a given line
Find the equation of a straight line perpendicular to a given line
topics.description: Calculate gradients and find equations of straight lines that are parallel or perpendicular to a given line.

subtopics: Understand the concept of a variable rate of change
Differentiate integer powers of x
Determine gradients and rates of change using differentiation
Find stationary points and turning points (maxima and minima)
Distinguish between maxima and minima by considering graph shape
Apply calculus to linear kinematics and simple practical problems
topics.description: Differentiate integer powers of x, find stationary points and turning points, and apply calculus to kinematics and practical problems.

subtopics: Understand and use internal and external intersecting chord properties
Recognise cyclic quadrilaterals and use their properties
Apply angle at centre is twice angle at circumference theorem
Apply angle in a semicircle is a right angle theorem
Apply angles in the same segment are equal theorem
Apply opposite angles of cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180° theorem
Apply the alternate segment theorem
topics.description: Understand and use circle theorems including intersecting chords, cyclic quadrilaterals, and the alternate segment theorem.

subtopics: Provide reasons using standard geometrical statements
Support numerical values for angles with geometrical reasoning
Construct chains of reasoning in geometrical contexts involving lines, polygons and circles
topics.description: Provide reasons using standard geometrical statements to support numerical values in any geometrical context involving lines, polygons and circles.

subtopics: Understand and use sine, cosine and tangent of obtuse angles
Understand and use angles of elevation and depression
Understand and use the sine rule for any triangle
Understand and use the cosine rule for any triangle
Use formula ½ab sin C for area of a triangle
topics.description: Use sine, cosine and tangent of obtuse angles, apply sine and cosine rules, and calculate areas of triangles using trigonometry.

topics.topicName: Pythagoras' theorem in 3D and advanced trigonometry
subtopics: Use Pythagoras' theorem in three dimensions
Apply trigonometrical methods to solve problems in three dimensions
Find the angle between a line and a plane in 3D

subtopics: Find perimeters of sectors of circles
Find areas of sectors of circles

subtopics: Find the surface area of a sphere using the relevant formula
Find the volume of a sphere using the relevant formula

Find the volume of a right circular cone using the relevant formula
topics.description: Find the surface area and volume of spheres and right circular cones using relevant formulae.

topics.topicName: Similarity in length, area and volume

Understand that volumes of similar figures are in the ratio of the cube of corresponding sides

subtopics: Understand that a vector has both magnitude and direction

Multiply vectors by scalar quantities
Add and subtract vectors
Calculate the modulus (magnitude) of a vector
Find the resultant of two or more vectors
Apply vector methods for simple geometrical proofs
topics.description: Understand vectors with magnitude and direction, use vector notation, perform operations with vectors, and apply vector methods for geometrical proofs.

subtopics: Construct and interpret histograms for continuous variables with unequal class intervals

topics.description: Construct and interpret histograms with unequal class intervals, and cumulative frequency diagrams.

topics.description: Estimate median and interquartile range from cumulative frequency diagrams, and calculate measures of spread.

subtopics: Draw and use tree diagrams
